ground_truth_summary: Artnight poster for acrylic painting event. Fiery red/orange horse over dark blue swirly background, 3 stylized trees on right (suspended in air). Top-left: VIC Health Club (hands+heart in laurel) and VIC Art Club (woman statue + inverted statue in laurel). Title lines: PAINT YOUR MASTERPIECE / ARTNIGHT / TUESDAY 14 APRIL | 17:00 | THE VIC. Bottom-right QR code with 'Scan to register' / 'vichealth.club'.

key_hits: Correctly identified ARTNIGHT title, PAINT YOUR MASTERPIECE tagline, event date/time/location
Identified VIC Health Club logo with hands/heart
Identified fiery red/orange horse against dark blue swirling background
Mentioned stylized trees with orange/yellow foliage on right
Correctly placed QR code and registration text at bottom-right
Noted impasto/post-impressionist Van Gogh style
key_misses: Misread VIC Art Club logo as 'VIC AC CLUB' instead of 'VIC Art Club'
Described right logo as 'three stylized human figures' instead of statues
Described bottom as 'solid black' background (ground truth says it's part of painting)
Did not mention trees being suspended in air
hallucinations: Claimed logo text reads 'VIC AC CLUB' (incorrect transcription)
Claimed bottom section has 'solid black background' — actually the painting continues
judgments.justification: Got the main event text and most visual elements right, but misread the Art Club logo text and described the bottom section as solid black rather than painted. Logo iconography for Art Club is wrong (figures vs statues).

judgments.justification: Comprehensive and accurate description. All verbatim text correct, visual elements well-described. Logo iconography is generic but acceptable. Best-in-class text accuracy.

notes: All 15 models successfully identified the dominant visual elements (fiery horse, swirling Van Gogh-like background, stylized trees on right) and got the verbatim text mostly correct. The most universal failure mode was the VIC Art Club logo iconography: ground truth describes statues (a woman statue next to an inverted statue), but every model described human silhouettes/figures instead. No model described the trees as 'suspended in air' — most assumed they rise from the ground. Most models also treated the bottom section as a 'solid dark blue/black footer', whereas the ground truth describes the entire poster area as the photograph of a painting. Only gemma4-12b significantly mangled the text ('VIC AC CLUB' instead of 'VIC Art Club'). The |think vs |nothink distinction for qwen3.5-9b shows minimal difference (both score 8); qwen3.6-27b|think slightly outperforms its nothink counterpart due to better positioning. Larger models (gemma4-26b-a4b, gemma4-31b, qwen3.6-27b, qwen3.6-35b-a3b) performed comparably to mid-tier models, suggesting this poster is well within capability of all tested models.
